caption: A light-colored dog is peeking out from behind beige curtains, with only its head and part of its front legs visible. Below the image, text reads, “If Your Dog Suffers From Bad Joints, Try This...” and indicates it is a promoted post by PETLAB CO.

This is an advertisement banner for Bitdefender, featuring images of three security product boxes — Bitdefender Antivirus Plus, Bitdefender Internet Security, and Bitdefender Total Security. A red "BEST DEAL" badge is placed over the product images, and a red "LEARN MORE" button is on the right side. The background shows two people looking at a computer screen with a blue-purple tint.

This is an advertisement for the "USANA CellSentials and USANimals™ Sweepstakes" promoted by the OZ brand, featuring images of three supplement bottles. A prominent blue "CLICK HERE" button invites viewers to participate, with the sponsorship by USANA noted below the button. The ad includes a disclaimer that the sweepstakes runs while supplies last.

The image displays a call to action with the text "HELP TO END ABORTION" and "SIGN THE DECLARATION," positioned above an image of a baby’s feet. The logo for "HUMAN COALITION" is visible in the upper right corner. The design uses a soft color palette with a blue banner for the call to action.

The image is a digital advertisement featuring a blue rectangular banner with the logo and name "cash flow frog" in the top right corner. It targets QuickBooks Online users, asking "QuickBooks Online User?" and offering to "Easily plan your cash flow" with a button for a "14 days free trial." Two computer screens displaying financial charts are shown on the right side of the banner.

The image displays an advertisement for Anodyne socks, showing a person's feet wearing the socks on a wooden floor. The ad includes the brand name "ANODYNE" and a "SHOP NOW" call-to-action button. A "Report Advertisement" link is visible below the ad.

The image displays a promotional graphic with the title "Iterate Reliably" and the brand name "MakerBot." Below the title, it states, "A Global Leader in 3D Printing, MakerBot™ Sees the Innovator in Everyone." A blue "LEARN MORE" button is positioned at the bottom.

This is a digital advertisement banner for AIG, featuring their logo on the left. The banner promotes three insurance plan options—Deluxe Plan, Preferred Plan, and Essential Plan—in bold yellow text. A call-to-action button labeled “GET A QUOTE” is positioned on the right side.

The image displays a media player interface with a blue play button centered. On the left side, bold text reads "PLAY HOT ARTISTS," followed by "ARIANA GRANDE" and "GOD IS A WOMAN" in smaller font. The background is plain white with a thin gray border.

This is an advertisement for SiriusXM, promoting a Select package subscription for $4.99 per month for 12 months. The image features a blue background with the SiriusXM logo, bold pricing text, and a "Get Started Now" button. It also includes a small "See Offer Details" note and a window control icon in the top-right corner.

A pair of glasses is partially submerged in water, creating ripples, against a solid blue background. The text "PANTONE® Color of the Year" is prominently displayed at the top, with a "SHOP NOW" button below it. The overall aesthetic emphasizes the color blue, suggesting it is the featured color for the year.

This is a promotional banner for a debate titled “#Antiwar Smackdown” featuring Scott Horton and William Kristol, dated May 11, 2020. Horton is labeled “con” and Kristol “pro” regarding the statement “Regime change is key to American foreign policy.” The banner includes their names, titles, and a “click for details” prompt.

A green and white digital ad promotes H&R Block, offering to save $10 or more by switching from TurboTax. The ad includes a yellow "File online now" button and the H&R Block logo. A small window control bar with minimize and close icons appears at the top right.

The image displays a text-based advertisement with a headline reading "The Latest Skinny Trend Melts 52 lbs in 28 Days." Below the headline, it states "SPONSOR: No diet or exercise required" followed by a red "[Full Story]" link. The text is presented on a plain gray background.

The image shows a close-up of Donald Trump, who is wearing a dark suit, white shirt, and red tie. He has his right hand raised near his ear and is looking slightly upward with a subtle expression. A text overlay at the bottom reads: “Historically Accurate Model Predicts a Surprising 2020 Election.”

This vertical banner advertises X-ES boards, highlighting their design for maximum performance and reliability in extreme environments. The image features close-up photographs of green circuit boards with various electronic components. A "VIEW BOARDS" button is prominently displayed, along with the X-ES logo at the bottom.

The image displays a close-up of an "AIRBAG" label on a textured surface, likely from a vehicle. Below it, a white banner reads: "If you were injured by a malfunctioning TAKATA airbag, you may be eligible for compensation." A red "LEARN MORE" link and the "gcg" logo are visible at the bottom.

A close-up shows fingers holding a white cotton pad against a person's skin, seemingly applying or wiping the area. The fingers have neatly trimmed, natural nails. Below the image, black text reads: "MD: If You Have Dark spots, Do This Immediately (It's Genius!).

The image shows a close-up of Melania Trump, looking slightly to her right with a neutral expression. She has blonde hair and is wearing makeup, including dark eyeliner and lipstick. At the bottom of the image, there is black text on a white background that reads, “Red Flags That Have Us Officially Worried About Melania Trump.”

The image shows a close-up of a roof covered in gray asphalt shingles arranged in a staggered pattern. In the background, bare trees and parts of adjacent rooftops are visible under a clear sky. Text at the bottom reads “Help for Homeowners with Old Roofing (Do This)” and “Modernize.”
A close-up shows fingers gently pressing a white cotton pad against human skin. Below the image, text reads: "MD: If You Have Dark spots, Do This Immediately (It’s Genius!)."

The image is a promotional graphic from Care Net, featuring a silhouette of a fetus against a dark red background. It urges viewers to "Take 30 seconds to sign a pledge against infanticide" with a prominent button that says "Sign the Pledge Today." The Care Net logo is displayed in the top left corner.

This is a promotional advertisement for Checkers, offering "Any 3 for R90 with card" on Oh My Goodness Ready Meals Assorted. The ad displays three packaged meals with cartoon monster characters on the labels, and notes that the deal was valid until 2 February 2020. A "VIEW DEALS" button is visible at the bottom.

This image displays a promotional graphic for "FabrIQ," featuring its logo and the tagline "The perfect workstation for creators." A prominent pink-outlined button labeled "Free Download" is centered below the tagline. The background shows a blurred screenshot of a financial dashboard with revenue figures and app icons.

The image is a promotional graphic encouraging users to support the Randolph Bourne Institute (Antiwar.com) through Amazon Smile. It features the word "Smile" in large red letters with the phrase "and make the world better" underneath, and includes a blue button prompting users to "CLICK to check/set" their charity. The top left shows "SMILE SHOP GIVE" and the bottom text specifies making the institute the chosen charity on Amazon Smile.

The image is a banner with a blue background and white text that reads “See Amazing Pro-Life Ads That Save Babies!” alongside a “WATCH” button. On the left side, there is a white box containing the “VirtueMedia” logo, featuring stylized blue lettering and white bird illustrations. The overall design is clean and uses contrasting colors for readability.

A promotional banner for a game titled "World War III" displays a soldier in military gear alongside a map of the Middle East. The banner poses the question “Dynamite into tinderbox?” and invites users to simulate a new Middle East scenario. A blue “ALLOW” button and a “NO THANKS” option are visible above the banner.

The image features a photo of Donald Trump speaking into a microphone, with a bold red "VOTE NOW" button overlaid at the bottom. Above the photo, white text on a black background reads, "Is Trump trying to start war with Iran to get reelected?". Below the red button, small text says "Report Advertisement".

The image shows a low-angle view of a subway platform, with a yellow safety line visible. A person's legs and feet are seen near the edge of the platform, with one person appearing to be falling or being pushed toward the train tracks. A portion of a train is visible in the background.

A user consent pop-up from "THE NATION" is displayed, offering options to "NO THANKS" or "ALLOW" for news and updates. To the right is a partially visible advertisement with the text "Digital life with security solutions" and a red "LEARN MORE" button. The top right corner shows a "DX" logo.

The image is an advertisement for Superblue Toothpaste, highlighting that it is fluoride-free and offering 50% off. The ad features a tube of the toothpaste angled against a dark background, with prominent text including “ORDER NOW.” The design uses a blue and white color scheme with bold fonts.

The image shows an advertisement for a poll asking “Should Trump Be Removed?” with options “Yes or No.” It invites users to cast their vote in a “National Poll” and includes the website “newsmax.com.” A button labeled “OPEN” is visible at the bottom.

Rows of pickup trucks are parked side by side on a gravel lot under a clear sky. The vehicles, viewed from the rear, include both dark and light-colored models stretching into the distance. A text overlay at the bottom reads: "Unsold Pickup Trucks Going For Pennies on The Dollar! Search For 2018 Best Pickup..."

The image displays two light blue triangular arrow icons on a white background. One arrow points left and is located on the far left edge, while the other points right and is on the far right edge. The icons appear to be navigation buttons, possibly for a carousel or slideshow interface.

The image displays an advertisement for Squarespace Website Builder, promoting its all-in-one platform for creating and managing professional websites. It features a prominent blue "OPEN" button and the brand name "Squarespace" below the main text. The ad is labeled as an "ADVERTISEMENT" at the top.

The image shows a close-up of a person’s cheek with visible dark spots and flaky skin, while a finger gently touches the area. To the right, there is a green checkmark icon next to bold text that reads, “MD: If You Have Dark spots, Do Immediately (It’s Genius)”. The overall layout suggests an advertisement or health tip related to skin concerns.

A man in a purple shirt is speaking directly to the camera, with a vibrant background of fresh vegetables and herbs. The image is overlaid with text that reads, “Top Gut Doctor: I Beg All Americans To Throw Out This Vegetable.” A sponsorship tag “S365 | Sponsored” appears at the bottom.

This is an advertisement for "RVL Reveal," a scalp infusion treatment featuring stem cell technology. The ad displays before-and-after images of hair growth and shows three product bottles. It mentions the treatment is from Beverly Hills celebrity/pro-athlete dermatologist, Dr. Nathan Newman, and is introduced by HealthylLooking.com.

The image is a digital advertisement for Yahoo! featuring the title "Early Signs & Symptoms Of Psoriatic Arthritis" with an illustration of feet. Below the title, smaller text reads "Research Psoriatic Arthritis Treatments," and a purple "LEARN MORE" button is positioned at the bottom right. The Yahoo! logo appears in purple to the left of the button.

This is a promotional advertisement for SiriusXM, featuring a blue background with the company logo and a call to action. The ad highlights a special offer to subscribe to the Select package for $4.99 per month for 12 months. A yellow "Get Started Now" button is prominently displayed at the bottom.

The image is an advertisement for the South Beach Diet featuring a smiling woman in a turquoise bikini on a sunny beach. It includes a testimonial from Jessie James Decker, who claims to have lost 25 lbs, with a call-to-action button to "Shop Plans." A disclaimer at the bottom notes an expected average weight loss of 1–2 lbs per week.

A woman holds up a cardboard sign with “Big PAY DAY” written on it. Below the image, text reads, “Washington Seniors On Medicare Are Getting a Big Pay Day.” A green checkmark icon is positioned to the left of the text.

The image displays an advertisement for the University of Cincinnati Online College of Nursing. It features a nurse using an otoscope to examine a patient’s ear. A red banner at the bottom prompts viewers to “SEE REQUIREMENTS >”.

The image is a close-up of Meghan Markle, showing her looking upward and to the side with a slightly parted mouth. She has dark, wavy hair pulled back loosely and is wearing a dark green garment with a textured collar. A text overlay at the bottom reads, “Unsettling Clip of Harry Scolding Meghan Comes to Light.”

The image features a man in a purple shirt speaking in front of a background filled with various vegetables, including tomatoes and leafy greens. Above him, bold text reads, "Top gut doctor warns: 'Throw out this vegetable immediately'". A red number "S365" is visible in the bottom left corner.

A close-up shows a person applying a round, beige-colored product to their cheek with their fingers. The person has red-painted fingernails and is wearing a red top. Text at the bottom reads, “How To Make Your Dark Spots Fade (It’s Genius)” and “Gundry MD.”

The image features a promotional graphic for the band Linkin Park, highlighting their song “In the End.” A large blue play button is centered over a stylized, gritty background with the band’s name and song title prominently displayed. The bottom right corner includes the Deezer logo and text attributing the design to “consumable.”

The image displays the Relais & Châteaux logo alongside the text "TREAT YOURSELF TO AN EXCEPTIONAL CULINARY JOURNEY." To the right, there's a close-up of an elegantly plated dish featuring a yellow sphere, greens, and other garnishes on a white surface. A small blue play button icon is visible in the top right corner.

A promotional advertisement for Melia Rewards featuring a vibrant tropical scene with a sailboat on blue-green ocean waters under a clear sky. The ad announces a "January Super Sale!" with up to 50% off and a bonus of 20,000 points, encouraging viewers to "Book Now." The text is overlaid in colorful fonts against the scenic background.

This is an advertisement for CallaBuy featuring two women modeling different sweaters. The top image shows a woman in a beige, geometric-patterned turtleneck sweater with button details on the side, while the bottom image shows a woman in a bright yellow V-neck sweater. The ad promotes "NOW UP TO 90% OFF" with a "VIEW MORE" button below.

A brown leather slip-on shoe with a thick, black rubber sole and orange stitching is displayed angled against a vintage book backdrop. The image features promotional text including "Comfy leather shoe" and a "65% OFF" discount, along with a "Banggood" logo.

This is an advertisement for Zenni eyewear featuring three women with different expressions and hairstyles, each wearing a distinct pair of glasses against varied pastel backgrounds. The headline "Eyewear for Every You" is prominently displayed above a red "SHOP NOW" button, with the Zenni logo at the bottom. The overall design highlights diverse styles of eyewear suitable for different personalities.

The image shows sliced bananas simmering in a silver saucepan on a stovetop. Below the image, there is a promotional text snippet that reads, "Top Cardiologist: This One Thing Will Properly..." followed by "Promoted | GUNDY MD SUP...".

The image features a red and gray split design with the logo “CatholicAdNet” in the top left. On the left side, bold white text reads “MORE CATHOLIC CUSTOMERS” above a “LEARN MORE” button. The right side shows silhouettes of people, one holding a cross, against a cloudy sky with sunlight breaking through.

This is a promotional advertisement for Nespresso, offering a limited edition free set of coffee coasters with the next order. The ad features a glass of coffee on one of the coasters, alongside a coffee capsule, with illustrations of the coaster designs. A "SHOP NOW" button and a note indicating the offer is Nespresso exclusive are also visible.

An advertisement for AARP features a woman in a swimsuit and swim cap flexing her bicep on a beach. The text promotes discovering a new you with AARP, offering only $12 for the first year with auto-renew. A prominent red button urges viewers to “JOIN NOW.”

The image displays a person in a bikini walking towards clear turquoise water under the shade of a thatched roof and hanging palm fronds. Above the scene, text reads “See The Best Adults Only Vacations For Under $1000! Search…” with a subheading “Seniors Vacations.” The overall setting suggests a tropical, secluded beach location.

The image displays an advertisement for "RUGGED SINGLE BOARD COMPUTERS" by XES, featuring a close-up view of a blue circuit board angled diagonally across a blue background. The text is presented in bold, uppercase letters, with a "Learn more →" call to action at the bottom left. A small window icon in the top right corner suggests this might be a screenshot from a digital interface.

A middle-aged man with gray hair, wearing a blue V-neck shirt, is shown in front of a bookshelf filled with books. The text overlay at the bottom reads “DOCTOR REVEALS: 3 UNBELIEVABLE FACTS ABOUT WEIGHT GAIN” and “LCR HEALTH.” He appears to be speaking or presenting, with his hands partially visible near his chest.

A close-up of a middle-aged to older man with a serious, somber expression. He has light skin, visible wrinkles, and is wearing formal attire with a white shirt and dark suit. The image includes a headline overlay reading, "The Outrage Over 'The Irishman' Is Getting Pretty Serious."

This is a digital advertisement for Bitdefender, featuring a group of smiling people in the background. A prominent green circle highlights a -56% discount, and the ad includes the tagline "Complete digital protection for more peace of mind." A red "BUY NOW" button is visible on the right side of the ad.

The image shows a large aircraft carrier sailing on the water, with its flight deck and superstructure visible. Below the image, text reads “Navy's New $13B Craft,” followed by a headline from CNET mentioning “Albert Einstein” tech and a claim about President Trump. A red circular button with a right-pointing arrow is on the lower right.

The image shows a close-up of a human ear with a drop of liquid being placed into the ear canal by a finger. Below the image, there is text that reads: "If You Have Ear Ringing Do This Immediately (Ends Tinnitus)." A small logo appears to the left of the text.

The image is a vertical collage of four distinct sections. The top section features a man in a suit gesturing while speaking, with the text “Bill O'Reilly Wants to Make You Rich.” The second section shows stacks of gold bars, accompanied by the text “The Little-Known IRS Law to Move to Gold.” The third section displays a close-up of a man’s face with the headline “Dr Phil Mourns As Tragedy Is Confirmed.” The bottom section depicts a man on a phone call with a play button overlay and text asking “millionaire yet? 2020.”

The image displays the "BitdefenderBOX" logo, with "Bitdefender" in black text and "BOX" in teal. To the left of the text is a blurry, abstract image featuring a brownish shape and a thin teal line. The overall design is minimalist and modern.

The image features a dark red, geometrically patterned background with a circular gray logo containing the letters "R9B" in red and white. Below the logo, the text "HUMAN-LED. TECHNOLOGY-ACCELERATED." is displayed in white, followed by a red button labeled "LEARN MORE." In the top right corner, a small blue and white icon resembling a play button is visible.

The image displays an advertisement for "TaxAct" with the tagline "Surprisingly Legal" beneath the brand name. The logo features "Tax" in red and "Act" in white, set against a dark blue background with abstract geometric shapes. A red "START NOW" button is positioned at the bottom.

The image shows a person pouring a yellow liquid into a white basin containing their feet. To the right, there is text that reads “If You Have Toenail Fungus, Do This Immediately (Genius!)” alongside a green checkmark icon and a green arrow button.

The image shows a close-up of a woman with brown hair, wearing a maroon beret-style hat adorned with a dark bow and pearl earrings. She is smiling and looking slightly to her right. The text overlay reads, “Kate's Bringing Back a Trend That Diana Absolutely Loathed.”

The image is an advertisement for "System 20" by OZ, promoting weight loss and health benefits. It features large text stating "Lose 20 pounds & reduce your risk of heart disease by 20% in 2020." On the right side, there's a close-up of a bowl filled with grilled chicken, Brussels sprouts, broccoli, and other vegetables.

The image features a soldier in camouflage gear walking away through a wooded area, with the bold text "SERVING THOSE WHO SERVE" prominently displayed. Below this, it advertises a "Special Discount for Military Members" from GEICO Military, accompanied by a button prompting viewers to "Start your free quote." The top right corner includes a small logo with "DX" inside a blue box.

A close-up photo shows a middle-aged to older woman with a concerned or worried expression, her hands clasped near her mouth. She has light-colored eyes and is wearing a gray shirt. The image includes a headline overlay that reads, “Washington Seniors On Medicare Are Getting A Big Pay Day,” with “Comparisons.org” at the bottom.

The image shows a close-up of a person's cheek with a patch of exfoliated skin, revealing a lighter layer underneath. A thumb is gently pressing on the textured skin near the cheekbone. Below the image, text reads: “MD: If You Have Dark spots, Do This Immediately (It’s Genius!)”.

The image displays the text "News Corp Australia" on a white background. "News Corp" is written in a black cursive font, while "Australia" is in a blue sans-serif font. A small blue "x" is visible in the top-right corner.

This is an advertisement image promoting a "Cleanse & Boost in 2020" product combo with a 50% discount. It displays five supplement bottles, including labels for "Winter Sun," "Nyxco," "Pollen Block," and "Peri Silver," arranged in front of a diagonal gray and white background. A green arrow and "Shop Now" text are positioned at the bottom right.

This is a political fundraising graphic urging donations to defeat Mitch McConnell, featuring black-and-white and color portraits of two individuals. It highlights a 350% match offer for $10 contributions and includes disclaimers noting it was paid for by Stop Republicans and credits Gage Skidmore (Flickr - edited) for the photos. The design uses bold text and contrasting colors to emphasize the urgent call to action.

The image displays a text snippet advertising a "27 Minute Back Pain Solution" claimed to be revealed by two golf pros. It includes a sponsor note prompting readers to try the solution to avoid needing painkillers again, with a red "[Full Story]" link. The text is presented in black and red against a light gray background.

This is an online advertisement banner featuring a woman in a pink bikini standing at the beach. The text reads "Great New Summer Bikinis" with the category "Summer Life" below. An "Open" button is visible on the right side of the ad.

A black, angular, modern car with large off-road tires is displayed on a red carpet in what appears to be an indoor showroom. The vehicle features a bold, geometric design with sharp lines and a prominent front grille. Text at the bottom of the image reads “8 Cars So Loaded It's Hard to Believe They Cost Under $20K. Research New Car Lease Deals.”

The image displays a media player interface with a black background and diagonal gray accents. It shows the song title "HOLA (REMIX)" by artist "DALEX," accompanied by a blue circular icon with a bar graph. A blue "FULL TRACK" button is visible on the right, and a "PLAY HOT ARTISTS" section is on the left.

The image displays a promotional graphic for a "WWIII simulation game," featuring a map of the United States of America highlighted in red. Below the map, text invites users to "Simulate and play out possible WWIII scenarios with all weaponry you can think of," accompanied by the logo for "Conflict Nations." A row of interface icons is visible beneath the map, suggesting gameplay controls.

A baby with a bald head and wide eyes is being held by an adult, wearing light blue striped overalls over a white long-sleeved shirt and white socks. The adult's hands gently support the baby, and a ring is visible on one finger. The image has a text overlay at the bottom that reads, "People Are Convinced Meghan and Harry Are Lying About Archie."

This is an advertisement banner featuring a pair of glasses floating on water against a blue background. The left side displays the text "PANTONE® Color of the Year," while the center has a "SHOP NOW" button. The right side shows the "ZENNI®" logo on a black background, with an "Ad" label in the top-right corner.

A person holds a blue Adidas shoebox featuring the brand's trefoil logo and three white stripes. Below the image, text reads, “This App Applies Every Coupon Code on the Internet to Your Cart...” and indicates it is a promoted ad by HONEY.

A woman with curly hair, wearing a green top, is smiling while holding a green blindfold over her eyes. To her left, green text reads "No surprises. Guaranteed." with a yellow button below saying "Get started today," and the H&R BLOCK logo at the bottom left.

The image shows two side-by-side illustrations of a woman’s face, both outlined with red triangles. The left face appears younger with smoother skin, while the right face shows signs of aging, such as wrinkles and sagging skin. Below the images, there is text that reads “Lighten Droopy Skin In 5 Mins” and a green button labeled “Do This.”

The image is an advertisement for Aeroflot, Russian Airlines, featuring a blue background with an airplane flying over clouds. Bold white text proclaims it as "THE YOUNGEST FLEET IN THE WORLD*" and notes it is "THE WORLD'S 4-STAR AIRLINE," accompanied by a gold seal. The Aeroflot logo and the Skytrax logo are visible in the upper left corner.

The image shows a close-up of a blonde woman wearing a bright yellow top and dangling earrings. She has a slightly concerned or exasperated facial expression, with her eyebrows slightly furrowed and lips pressed together. A text overlay at the bottom reads “Fox News Hosts Who Lost It On Live TV.”

The image displays a promotional graphic for Square Online Store, featuring the headline "Professional. Fast. Free." and a subheading that reads "Streamline Your Online Operations and Work More Efficiently. Create Your Online Store." A prominent blue "LEARN MORE" button is positioned at the bottom.

The image displays a pop-up window for the Squarespace Website Builder, featuring the title "Squarespace Website Builder" and the tagline "Make and manage your own professional website with Squarespace's all-in-one platform." Below this text is a prominent black "OPEN" button. The window has a close button in the top right corner.

This is a promotional banner for Bitdefender, featuring the company's logo on the left. A red circular badge labeled "BEST DEAL" is positioned centrally, and to the right, the text reads, "Protect your digital life with Bitdefender security solutions." A red "LEARN MORE" button is located on the far right side of the banner.

The image displays a digital graphic promoting the "2020 March for Life Survey." It features a background photo of a crowd of smiling individuals, many wearing winter hats, holding signs with messages like "I AM THE LIFE GENERATION." A prominent green button at the bottom reads "BEGIN SURVEY."

A woman wearing a straw sun hat is shown with sunscreen applied under her nose, looking upward with her eyes closed against a backdrop of ocean and sky. The image includes text at the bottom reading “8 TIPS TO AVOID SUN SPOTS” and “BESTALLOVER.” Her hand gently touches the brim of her hat.

A woman with short blonde hair is shown in profile, inserting a small white object into her ear. She is wearing a light-colored top, and the background is plain and light-colored. Text at the bottom of the image reads "If You Have Ear Ringing Do This Immediately (Ends Tinnitus)" by "Life Now Naturals."

The image displays a promotional graphic with a close-up of a baby's feet in the center. At the top right, the logo and name "HUMAN COALITION" are visible. Large text reads "HELP TO END ABORTION," with a blue button below that says "SIGN THE DECLARATION."

The image displays an advertisement for Zenni, featuring the slogan "Eyewear for Every You." It shows three women with different facial expressions and eyewear styles against colored backgrounds. The Zenni logo is prominently displayed on a black background to the right.

The image displays an advertisement for a $4.95 30-day trial of Equifax Complete™ Premier, which includes credit monitoring and identity theft protection. It features icons representing credit monitoring with a phone number and ID theft protection with a shield, along with a "Get Started" button. The Equifax logo is visible at the bottom.

The image displays three footprints—silver, gold, and gold—arranged in a row against a white background with starburst patterns behind them. Below the footprints, bold text reads "PRECIOUS FEET MAKE AN IMPACT!" followed by smaller text identifying it as "The International Pro-Life Symbol."

A hand holds a fan of U.S. dollar bills against a plain background. Below the image, text reads "Savvy Americans Are Opening These Checking Accounts" with a "Learn More" button. The Yahoo! logo is displayed at the bottom.

The image features a red shield with a white checkmark at its center, set against a background of blue binary code. Below the graphic, bold text reads "(2018) 10 Best FREE Antivirus Software To Protect Your PC!" followed by a smaller line repeating the same title and adding "(See inside)". At the bottom, "Top 10 FREE Antivirus" is displayed in white text.

The image shows several cooked beets on a white plate, with purple juice staining the plate's surface, set on a wooden table next to a knife. Below the photo, there is a text overlay that reads, “If You Have Toenail Fungus, Do This Immediately (Genius!)” and “Clear Nail Plus.”

The image is an advertisement for a white T-shirt featuring a graphic of five U.S. presidents holding an American flag, with the text "THE DEPLORABLES" printed below. The top right corner shows the "PATRIOT DEPOT" logo, and a red banner at the bottom declares "ON SALE NOW!" with arrows pointing right. The text "Advertisement" is centered at the top.
